channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Priorities for advancing artificial intelligence in inflammatory bowel disease: a technology update.
item.description: Gastrointestinal endoscopy - Review - Artificial intelligence (AI), encompassing various methods that enable computers to process complex data and perform tasks previously requiring human input, is set to play a pivotal role in the fut...

item.title: INTELCAPE: A Deep Learning-Powered System for Automated, High-Accuracy Crohn's Disease Diagnosis via Capsule Endoscopy.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Capsule endoscopy (CE) is a non-invasive technique for diagnosing Crohn's disease (CD); however, manual interpretation of CE videos is time-consuming and error-prone. We developed an artificial int...

item.title: A lactylation modification-related prediction model for the diagnosis of ulcerative colitis based on machine learning.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Lactylation modification serves as a critical link between metabolic reprogramming and epigenetic regulation, playing a significant role in the progression of both malignant tumors and inflammatory...

item.title: Evaluating the impact of therapeutic drug monitoring strategies on clinical outcomes in paediatric inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - The increasing incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in paediatric populations underscores the importance of effective management strategies. Anti-tumor necrosis factor (anti-TNF) agents, s...

item.title: TET1 deficiency amplifies macrophage inflammatory signaling associated with Crohn's disease.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - To define the role of Ten-Eleven Translocation (TET) proteins in Crohn's disease (CD)-associated inflammation through integrative human and mechanistic studies. item.title: Immunoinformatic-based design of a multi-epitope subunit vaccine against Ruminococcus torques using subtractive proteomics and molecular dynamics simulations.

item.title: Adverse Events and Safety Outcomes Associated With Chemotherapy for Inflammatory Bowel Disease-associated Gastrointestinal Cancers.
item.description: Anticancer research - Management of inflammatory bowel disease-associated gastrointestinal cancers, including Crohn's disease-associated cancer and ulcerative colitis-associated cancer, remains challenging because of co...

item.title: STING pathway contributes to Steroid-Hyporesponsive Lung Inflammation in DSS-induced colitis mice model.
item.description: PloS one - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), a chronic inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract, is well recognized for triggering extraintestinal manifestations, including pulmonary complications. Emerging...

item.title: Managing Acute Severe Ulcerative Colitis in the Older Patient: A Growing Concern.
item.description: Drugs & aging - Review - Since the prevalence of older patients with ulcerative colitis is increasing, hospitalizations for severe disease in this age group are expected to rise. Older patients are more likely to present w...

item.title: Use of magnetically controlled capsule endoscopy in inflammatory pediatric Crohn's disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - This study aimed to evaluate the clinical utility of magnetically controlled capsule endoscopy (MCCE) in the evaluation and management of pediatric Crohn's disease (CD). A single-center retrospecti...

item.title: The prognostic role of magnetic resonance enterography at diagnosis in paediatric isolated ileocaecal Crohn's disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - We aimed at identifying magnetic resonance enterography (MRE) predictors of surgical intervention and anti-tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-α) initiation in a paediatric cohort of patients newly di...

item.title: Diagnostic value of CT enterography combined with inflammatory indicators in active Crohn's disease.

item.title: Diagnosis of inflammatory bowel disease following symptomatic faecal immunochemical testing - A population based cohort study.

item.title: Discordant Clostridioides difficile testing as a predictor of inflammatory bowel disease therapy escalation.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - In patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), Clostridioides difficile infection (CDI) presents with clinical features indistinguishable from the underlying disease. Sequential polymerase chai...

item.title: Exposure to per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances and inflammatory bowel disease: review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Journal of exposure science & environmental epidemiology - Review - Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are stable chemicals that are well preserved in the environment and persist in the human body. PFAS have been associated with inflammatory bowel disease (...

item.title: Distinct clinicopathological features of IgA nephropathy associated with Crohn's disease: comparison with ulcerative colitis and non-IBD IgA nephropathy.
item.description: Clinical and experimental nephrology - IgA nephropathy (IgAN) is increasingly recognized as a complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), particularly Crohn's disease (CD). Recent studies suggest that mucosal immune dysregulation ...

item.title: ORMDL3: from an asthma susceptibility gene to multi-disease associations.
item.description: Frontiers in genetics - Review - The ORMDL3 protein, encoded by the ORMDL3 gene, functions as a transmembrane protein in the endoplasmic reticulum. Initially identified through its genetic link to asthma susceptibility, ORMDL3 pla...

item.title: Endarachne binghamiae Extract Alleviates Colitis by Suppressing NLRP3 Inflammasome Activation via Regulation of NOX-iNOS Crosstalk.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is triggered by genetic predisposition and chronic inflammation, with aberrant activation of the innate immune complex NLRP3 inflammasome playing a pivotal role in ...
